programme.title: Earth
programme.desc: Presenter and naturalist Chris Packham will present the most important moments from the existence of the Blue Planet.The creators will show, among other things, extreme climate changes and continental collisions.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Islands: Nature's Wild Laboratories
programme.desc: The creators of the series take viewers on a journey through the unique ecosystems of islands, from the Arctic to the tropics.The trek includes Borneo, the Okavango Delta, Vancouver Islands, Baffin Island, K'gari and Sri Lanka.

programme.title: The World's Deadliest Weather
programme.desc: Moving accounts of people who survived confrontations with the forces of nature.This series includes, among others:memories of earthquakes in Turkey and Syria and fires raging in Los Angeles.

programme.title: How to Boost Your Brain Power
programme.desc: The authors of the document present how, thanks to the latest research, expert opinions and lifestyle changes, you can improve the functioning of your brain.

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The creators of the series go on a train journey to various places around the world.During each expedition, they meet people who live in specific areas or work on the most unique railway routes.They will also discover the wonders of nature that can be admired from the train windows.They will talk to engineers responsible for maintaining the lines and historians who will talk about the circumstances of their construction and the difficulties their builders faced.Viewers will be able to admire the landscapes of Norway, New Zealand, Canada, Switzerland, Wales and Spain.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Earth
programme.desc: Presenter and naturalist Chris Packham will present the most important moments from the existence of the Blue Planet.The creators will show, among other things, extreme climate changes and continental collisions.

programme.title: Attenborough and the Giant Dinosaur
programme.desc: In 2014, a large bone was discovered in Patagonia, the size of which amazed all scientists.It was thought that it could have belonged to the largest dinosaur that ever walked the Earth.David Attenborough sets off with a camera to Argentina to work with scientists on the complex process of reconstructing the largest reptile of all time.Specialists managed to assemble a 37-meter skeleton from 220 discovered bones, and using computer graphics to recreate not only the entire organism and its mechanics, but also the conditions in which it lived.The film shows how it is possible that such a huge dinosaur appeared on Earth, and also explains the reasons for its extinction.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The documentaries present a side of the Galapagos Islands that is not widely known.They explain why they are so important to understanding evolutionary history.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: In each episode, David Attenborough presents two cases of natural curiosities that have something in common.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The documentaries present a side of the Galapagos Islands that is not widely known.They explain why they are so important to understanding evolutionary history.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Ben Fogle: Return to the Wild 2
programme.desc: Traveler Ben Fogle revisits the participants of the "Life in the Wasteland" program.These people have in common the fact that years ago they decided to abandon their previous existence in favor of living in the bosom of nature, often in a remote area and in extremely harsh conditions.Ben will check, among others:what happened to a former war correspondent who became a grizzly bear expert.He will also revisit a former financier who is now living the life of Tarzan in a swampy area in Georgia.The presenter will find out whether these people are satisfied with their choices.

programme.title: Islands: Nature's Wild Laboratories
programme.desc: Baffin is the largest island in the Canadian Arctic.In summer, when the ice recedes, animals must adapt to new conditions.Polar bears hunt seals off the shore and climb cliffs in search of nesting birds.The camera also follows arctic foxes raising their cubs and herds of white belugas.There is also the first-ever footage of a narwhal with two horns.This extraordinary material shows how strongly climate change affects this harsh yet vibrant ecosystem.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Sue Perkins Into Alaska
programme.desc: Actress Sue Perkins sets off on a journey through Alaska, the largest American state, famous for its harsh climate and wildlife.The expedition begins in the most populated city, Anchorage.

programme.title: Griff's Canadian Adventure
programme.desc: Welsh comedian and traveler Griff Rhys Jones goes on a journey through Canada, the second largest country in the world.He visits picturesque mountain ranges and vast prairies, as well as metropolises and towns located in the Arctic wilderness.He begins to understand the impact of its rich history on the current state and what determines the originality of individual provinces.Griff visits places in Canada such as Newfoundland on the east coast and Vancouver on the Pacific Ocean.In each episode, the comedian takes on a challenge specific to the region he visits.

programme.title: The Secret Genius of Modern Life
programme.desc: Dr. Hannah Fry will present interesting facts about everyday objects.We will find out, among others:What could bulletproof glass, a French serial killer and the CIA have to do with the creation of the passport?

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: In each episode, David Attenborough presents two cases of natural curiosities that have something in common.

programme.title: Earth
programme.desc: Presenter and naturalist Chris Packham will present the most important moments from the existence of the Blue Planet.The creators will show, among other things, extreme climate changes and continental collisions.

programme.title: Life Below Zero: Canada
programme.desc: The creators of the series present the everyday life of a group of daredevils struggling with wildlife in various regions of northern Canada during a frosty winter.Johnatan Granier will join the program in the second season.

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The creators of the series go on a train journey to various places around the world.During each expedition, they meet people who live in specific areas or work on the most unique railway routes.They will also discover the wonders of nature that can be admired from the train windows.They will talk to engineers responsible for maintaining the lines and historians who will talk about the circumstances of their construction and the difficulties their builders faced.Viewers will be able to admire the landscapes of Norway, New Zealand, Canada, Switzerland, Wales and Spain.

programme.title: Martin Clunes: Islands of the Atlantic
programme.desc: Actor Martin Clunes continues his journey around the world's islands, this time visiting the Atlantic.There he gets to know the locals, their customs and meets unique animals inhabiting these areas.

programme.title: Islands: Nature's Wild Laboratories
programme.desc: Baffin is the largest island in the Canadian Arctic.In summer, when the ice recedes, animals must adapt to new conditions.Polar bears hunt seals off the shore and climb cliffs in search of nesting birds.The camera also follows arctic foxes raising their cubs and herds of white belugas.There is also the first-ever footage of a narwhal with two horns.This extraordinary material shows how strongly climate change affects this harsh yet vibrant ecosystem.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Wild Catch
programme.desc: The famous adventurer Cyril Chauquet and his team once again set off on a dangerous expedition.Their goal is to find and study the most mysterious and dangerous fish species inhabiting various parts of the world.The team faces predators such as caimans and rays, meets illegal fishermen and traverses treacherous rivers.During this extraordinary journey, they not only look for new discoveries, but also help protect valuable habitats of endangered species.

programme.title: Earth
programme.desc: Presenter and naturalist Chris Packham will present the most important moments from the existence of the Blue Planet.The creators will show, among other things, extreme climate changes and continental collisions.

programme.title: Australia's Health Revolution with Michael Mosley
programme.desc: Dr. Michael Mosley and physiologist Ray Kelly challenge people suffering from type 2 diabetes with an eating plan that can improve the symptoms of their disease in just a few days.Viewers will find out whether a radical diet based on numerous scientific studies can be the key to controlling the diabetes epidemic in Australia.For the good of humanity, Mosley also decides to experience the effects of a certain diet for himself.The doctor follows a menu consisting of sweet carbohydrates and saturated fats.He wants to make viewers aware of the impact of processed food on human health.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Sue Perkins Into Alaska
programme.desc: Actress Sue Perkins sets off on a journey through Alaska, the largest American state, famous for its harsh climate and wildlife.The expedition begins in the most populated city, Anchorage.

programme.title: Griff's Canadian Adventure
programme.desc: Welsh comedian and traveler Griff Rhys Jones goes on a journey through Canada, the second largest country in the world.He visits picturesque mountain ranges and vast prairies, as well as metropolises and towns located in the Arctic wilderness.He begins to understand the impact of its rich history on the current state and what determines the originality of individual provinces.Griff visits places in Canada such as Newfoundland on the east coast and Vancouver on the Pacific Ocean.In each episode, the comedian takes on a challenge specific to the region he visits.

programme.title: The Secret Genius of Modern Life
programme.desc: Dr. Hannah Fry will present interesting facts about everyday objects.We will find out, among others:What could bulletproof glass, a French serial killer and the CIA have to do with the creation of the passport?

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: In each episode, David Attenborough presents two cases of natural curiosities that have something in common.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The documentaries present a side of the Galapagos Islands that is not widely known.They explain why they are so important to understanding evolutionary history.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Sue Perkins Into Alaska
programme.desc: Actress Sue Perkins sets off on a journey through Alaska, the largest American state, famous for its harsh climate and wildlife.The expedition begins in the most populated city, Anchorage.

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: A documentary series presenting extraordinary places on Earth.

programme.title: Life Below Zero 8
programme.desc: Despite climate change, the Hailstons do not give up their traditional lifestyle.Sue Aikens creates new tools, and Jessie Holmes and his dogs try to acclimatize to new areas.

programme.title: Life Below Zero 8
programme.desc: Despite climate change, the Hailstons do not give up their traditional lifestyle.Sue Aikens creates new tools, and Jessie Holmes and his dogs try to acclimatize to new areas.

programme.title: Treasures Decoded
programme.desc: The creators of the series conduct archaeological investigations into unsolved mysteries of antiquity.The emergence of new evidence gives researchers further opportunities to solve each of them.In each episode, world-class experts from various fields focus on a different archaeological object and search for the truth about its origin.Viewers will learn the latest scientific theories, dramatic stories and surprising answers about the real Tower of Babel, the Egyptian plagues, Stonehenge and the city of Petra.

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: A documentary series presenting extraordinary places on Earth.

programme.title: Wonders of the Moon with Dara Ó Briain
Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Where the Wild Men Are with Ben Fogle
programme.desc: Traveler Ben Fogle learns the stories of people who decided to abandon their previous city life in favor of existence in the bosom of nature.The program's characters often live in remote areas and in places with extremely harsh conditions.In the ninth season of the program, Ben travels to Portugal, where he visits Alex, who has abandoned a lucrative career in trading to hide in a mountain hideout.The traveler also goes to the Irish wilderness, where an elderly couple, George and Bettina, live.

programme.title: Sue Perkins Into Alaska
programme.desc: Actress Sue Perkins sets off on a journey through Alaska, the largest American state, famous for its harsh climate and wildlife.The expedition begins in the most populated city, Anchorage.

programme.title: Treasures Decoded
programme.desc: The creators of the series conduct archaeological investigations into unsolved mysteries of antiquity.The emergence of new evidence gives researchers further opportunities to solve each of them.In each episode, world-class experts from various fields focus on a different archaeological object and search for the truth about its origin.Viewers will learn the latest scientific theories, dramatic stories and surprising answers about the real Tower of Babel, the Egyptian plagues, Stonehenge and the city of Petra.

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: A documentary series presenting extraordinary places on Earth.

programme.title: Once Upon A Time In Space
programme.desc: The creators of the four-part series take viewers on a journey through the history of the conquest of space.They give a voice to those who were involved in groundbreaking events.They show known stories from a new, very personal perspective.Astronauts, engineers and NASA employees, as well as specialists from the Russian space program, speak in front of the cameras.Their memories reveal a picture of great rivalry between superpowers, technological breakthroughs and emotions accompanying crossing new borders.A Great Leap for Mankind The first episode of this chronicle focuses on American astronauts who blazed unknown trails.The adversities they faced reflect the prejudices of the era.In 1978, black Ron McNair and Anna Fisher were selected by NASA to fly the newly unveiled Space Shuttle.For the first time in the history of the agency, candidates began to be accepted regardless of race or gender.Interviews and archival photos reveal the atmosphere of an era marked by fierce rivalry between the United States and the Soviet Union.In addition to moments of triumph, filmmakers recall tragedies such as the Challenger disaster.They show how the future of space flights is shaped in the context of history.

programme.title: Where the Wild Men Are with Ben Fogle
programme.desc: In the next season of the program, traveler Ben Fogle visits snowboarding legend Mike Basich, who abandoned the sport and started a new life in a self-built cabin in the Sierra Nevada mountains.He also goes to visit Bette, who has moved to the Australian outback and is struggling with snakes and floods.Rhona gave up her career in Hollywood and chose a lonely life in the bosom of nature in Uruguay.Ben helps her take care of the horses and discovers that thanks to her new life, the woman has felt peace and satisfaction for years.
